RuntimeError: Index tensor must have the same number of dimensions as self tensor

llm_operation.reason: PyTorch torch.scatter_add requires the index tensor to have the same number of dimensions as the input tensor when dim is specified, but the current test case uses a 1-D index ([5]) with a 2-D input ([10,5]) and dim=0 — this is invalid per PyTorch's scatter_add signature. According to PyTorch docs, for scatter_add(input, dim, index, src), the index must be of the same rank as input, and each dimension except dim must match input's corresponding dimension; index.shape[dim] must equal src.shape[dim], and other dimensions of index must match input's (not src's). Here, input is [10,5], dim=0 → index must be [5,5] (to match input.shape[1]==5 in non-dim dims), not [5]. Paddle's paddle.scatter has different semantics: it expects 1-D index for scattering updates into x along axis 0 (by default), and does not require index rank == x rank. Thus the original case is malformed for PyTorch. Repair: make index shape [5,5] to satisfy torch.scatter_add constraints while keeping total indexed elements (5) and broadcast-compatible updates shape [5,5]. Also ensure src matches: for dim=0, src.shape must equal index.shape, i.e., [5,5]. This aligns with PyTorch doc requirement: 'index must have the same number of dimensions as input, and all dimensions except dim must match input'. Since dim=0, index[1] must = input[1] = 5 → index shape = [?,5]; and index.shape[0] must equal src.shape[0] = 5 → index shape = [5,5].
